do_not: Do not silently delete the repeated event; the explicit pseudo-chain shape may be intentional.
diagnostics.message: Combo trigger repeats event 'Root.Waiting.Request'; this is legal but usually redundant.

recommended_actions.rationale: Check whether the second event was meant to be a different event.
Keep it only if the explicit two-hop pseudo chain is intentional.

repair_guidance: Repeated identical event terms are presence-based and are usually redundant.
If there is no evidence that the explicit two-hop pseudo chain is intentional, reducing the duplicated term to one event is the smallest semantic repair.
diagnostics.summary: The combo trigger contains the same resolved event twice, for example ``E1 + E1``. This is legal but normally redundant because one cycle's input event set already contains ``E1``.

do_not: Do not add a dummy guard reference only to silence the diagnostic.
diagnostics.message: Variable 'ready' does not affect any transition guard.

recommended_actions.rationale: Remove the variable and related writes if it was speculative scaffolding.
Add the missing data-flow path if the variable should affect a transition decision.

suggested_fix.rationale: Remove the declaration-only variable because it has no DSL reads or writes.

repair_guidance: A declaration-only variable may be speculative scaffolding.
Remove it only when no guard, assignment, abstract action, or external integration intent needs it.
diagnostics.summary: This variable does not participate in model decisions. It is dead from the DSL's guard-affect data-flow perspective.

do_not: Do not add a meaningless event just to silence this info.
diagnostics.message: Transition 'Root.Accepted' -> '[*]' has no event or guard.

recommended_actions.rationale: Keep it when the state should immediately advance.
Add the missing event or guard if the transition should wait.

diagnostics.summary: The transition is not event-triggered and has no guard. This may be a deliberate unconditional fall-through, so treat it as context rather than a fix target.

repair_protocol.goal: repair the FCSTM model with the smallest semantic source change
rules: Make the smallest source edit that preserves the modeler's apparent intent.
Use diagnostic source/provenance before choosing a fix; inspect-static warnings are not solver proofs.
Do not mechanically stack all suggested actions when multiple diagnostics refer to the same region.
Do not delete states or transitions unless the report explicitly says the element is unused and the design intent supports deletion.
A repair should clear all error and warning diagnostics; info diagnostics may remain when the model intent supports them.
If changing a guard into an event-only transition makes a variable declaration unused, remove that variable or keep a real guard-affecting data-flow path.
